titleStmt.title: Report by the Executive Committee on Economic Foreign Policy Entitled “United States–Philippine Trade Preferences in relation to General Commercial Policy”.

div.note: Records of the Executive Secretariat, Lot 122, Box 20

head.hi: Report by the Executive Committee on Economic Foreign Policy
head.note: Entitled “United States–Philippine Trade Preferences in relation to General Commercial Policy”.

div.p:
According to a press release issued by the Philippine Government on February 15, 1945 the Filipino group of the CommissionFilipino Rehabilitation Commission. submitted to Senator Millard E. Tydings, Chairman of the Commission, a proposal under which there would be free trade between the United
States and the Philippines except that the basic quotas established by the Independence Act,Approved March 24, 1934; 48 Stat. 456. as amended by the Act of August 7, 193953 Stat. (pt. 2) 1226. would be retained. This arrangement would continue in force for twenty years after independence is declared and would remain in force thereafter “unless either the United States Government or the Government of the Philippines will desire to change, modify, or terminate the arrangement, in which case, a five year prior notice shall be given after the fifteenth year from the date of the proclamation of Philippine independence.”
